meta.name: convergence_harness
meta.purpose: The side-by-side CONVERGENCE comparator harness. A Fixture Feeder emits the current candidate + a fixed reference into CompareDiff; the scalar distance d feeds a Convergence Gate (a Context gate over a State round-counter) whose `fire` is `d <= epsilon`; on any round the Iterate Action advances the fixture and a Report Message narrates the round. The whole point is that d is a WIRED, inspectable output on every round, so a driver can assert d DECREASES (converging) or FAILS to decrease (non-converging). The candidate is fed as a param (fixture_candidate) so a driver hot-loads it per round as a diff splice; reference + epsilon are params.

params.fixture_candidate: the candidate value for THIS round (hot-loaded per round by the driver)
params.fixture_reference: the fixed target the candidate is converging toward
params.epsilon: convergence threshold: the gate fires when d <= epsilon

nodes.note: Fixture Feeder OUT: the candidate value for this round. The driver diff-splices params.value each round to feed a sequence (10 -> 5 -> 2 -> ... converging, or a non-monotone sequence for the failure contract).
Reference slot: the fixed target the candidate approaches.
The comparator: d = |candidate - reference|. The load-bearing output the harness measures each round.
Gate predicate: d <= epsilon. The `fire` boolean the Convergence Gate reads.

Convergence Gate: a Context `gate` scope over a State round-counter. The WHOLE scope only propagates while its implicit `enabled` input (wired from `converged`) is truthy; otherwise `count` is null (the gate has not fired). So a non-null `count` == the gate FIRED at convergence.

Iterate Action: on every round advance the fixture (a WorldAction `log` op — the harmless declared effect proving the sink round-trips). A host that registers a real `fixture.advance` op honours it; here it is a declared receipt.
Report: one Message record narrating the round (a wire-able, inspectable data node — the harness's human-readable trace).
